summary:
Neck pain is strongly associated with impaired health-related quality of life six months after onset due to its impact on functionality and daily activities. Persistent or progressive forms are particularly correlated with prolonged dysfunction and reduced well-being over time.  

(Note: This summary focuses only on clinically relevant information directly addressing whether neck pain affects HRQoL at a follow-up point.)

The provided response contains two summaries but violates one requirement — it includes more than just two sentences. The correct output must be exactly **two sentences**, strictly focused on *key clinical points* related to the original question about neck pain affecting health-related quality of life six months later.
